title: Polystichum lineare
intro: Polystichum lineare, also known as the Chinese sword fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the genus Polystichum and is native to East Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, and it has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Polystichum lineare include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Polystichum lineare is classified as a species of the genus Polystichum and the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. The order and family classification places it in the Polypodiales order and Dryopteridaceae family. This classification is based on its characteristics and shared traits with other species in the same family. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and the biological diversity of the plant.

The plant's fronds have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems.
